jurisdictions.name: UAE
jurisdictions.notes: Primary market. Fully operational with VARA regulatory coverage. Focus on continuous compliance and market expansion.

jurisdictions.name: Saudi Arabia
jurisdictions.notes: Assessment in progress. Regulatory framework for tokenized assets under development by CMA. Engaging with local legal counsel.

jurisdictions.name: Kuwait
jurisdictions.notes: Initial assessment underway. Small but wealthy market with high potential for tokenized real estate.

jurisdictions.name: Qatar
jurisdictions.notes: Not yet assessed. Potential market for 2027 expansion.

jurisdictions.name: Bahrain
jurisdictions.notes: Ready to enter. CBB has progressive fintech regulations. Local partnership identified.

jurisdictions.name: Oman
jurisdictions.notes: Partner-led approach through existing relationships. Local partner identified for regulatory navigation.

jr1a2b3c4-1901-4000-8000-000000000001.mandatoryDisclaimer: Tokenized ownership represents proportional beneficial interest in the underlying asset. Legal structure is an SPV.
Secondary market trading is subject to VARA regulations and platform terms. Liquidity is not guaranteed.
Distributions are based on actual rental income after expenses. Past distributions do not guarantee future payments.

entryMemos.title: Saudi Arabia Market Entry Assessment
entryMemos.executiveSummary: Saudi Arabia represents the largest GCC market outside UAE with strong demand for real estate investment. CMA is developing digital asset regulations. Market entry recommended in phased approach.
entryMemos.recommendation: Phased entry beginning with institutional partnerships, followed by retail investor access once CMA framework matures.
entryMemos.evidenceHighlights: Saudi real estate market valued at $500B+. CMA Fintech Lab enables sandbox testing. Strong government push for financial innovation under Vision 2030. High demand for Sharia-compliant investment products.
risksAndAssumptions: CMA regulatory framework for tokenized assets not yet finalized
Local partner required for Sharia compliance certification
Cultural considerations for marketing financial products
nextActions30_60_90: Engage Saudi legal counsel for regulatory analysis (30 days)
Initiate CMA Fintech Lab sandbox application (60 days)
Identify and evaluate local partners (90 days)

entryMemos.title: Bahrain Market Entry Assessment
entryMemos.executiveSummary: Bahrain has the most progressive fintech regulatory environment in the GCC after UAE. CBB's regulatory sandbox and crypto-asset module provide clear framework for tokenized assets.
entryMemos.recommendation: Direct market entry via partnership with local fintech. No regulatory blockers identified.
entryMemos.evidenceHighlights: CBB Crypto-Asset Module provides clear regulatory pathway. Bahrain Fintech Bay ecosystem. Strong English proficiency reducing localization costs.
risksAndAssumptions: Smaller market size compared to UAE or Saudi
nextActions30_60_90: Finalize partnership agreement with local fintech (30 days)
Submit regulatory notification to CBB (45 days)
Soft launch with selected properties (90 days)

entryMemos.title: Kuwait Market Entry Assessment
entryMemos.executiveSummary: Kuwait market shows potential for tokenized real estate given high wealth concentration. However, CMA Kuwait regulatory framework for digital assets is still developing.
entryMemos.recommendation: Monitor regulatory development and maintain preliminary partner discussions. Defer active entry until regulatory clarity improves.
entryMemos.evidenceHighlights: High per-capita wealth in Kuwait. Strong demand for alternative investments. Limited exposure to real estate tokenization.
risksAndAssumptions: CMA Kuwait has not yet published digital asset regulations
Political dynamics may impact market entry timing
nextActions30_60_90: Monitor CMA Kuwait regulatory developments (30 days)
Prepare preliminary market entry plan (90 days)

claimReference.condition: Requires Sharia board approval for each tokenized asset. Must include Sharia compliance certificate.
claimReference.mandatoryDisclaimer: Sharia compliance certification issued by [Sharia Board Name]. Investors should conduct their own due diligence.
claimReference.citation: UAE Federal Law No. 6 of 1985 (Civil Transactions Law) - Sharia compliance standards

claimReference.condition: Due diligence process must be documented and available for regulatory review upon request.
claimReference.mandatoryDisclaimer: Due diligence conducted by Blocks and its appointed third-party experts. Due diligence does not eliminate investment risk.

claimReference.condition: Applicable only in designated freehold areas as defined by Dubai Land Department. Requires DLD registration.
claimReference.mandatoryDisclaimer: Freehold ownership rights are subject to Dubai Land Department regulations and designated freehold area designations.
claimReference.citation: Dubai Law No. 7 of 2006 (Real Property Registration) - Freehold areas

claimReference.claim: Real estate investment for non-Saudi nationals
claimReference.condition: Saudi Arabia restricts real estate ownership by non-GCC nationals in certain areas. Tokenization structure requires legal review.
claimReference.citation: Saudi Arabian Investment Law - Real estate ownership restrictions

claimReference.condition: Must operate under CBB Crypto-Asset Module regulations. Regular reporting and compliance audits required.
claimReference.mandatoryDisclaimer: Regulated by the Central Bank of Bahrain under the Crypto-Asset Module. Investors should review the relevant regulations.
